Harden LLM router resilience and docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

perf(maglev): extend int32 truncation to Generic arithmetic ops

Recognize Generic bitwise/shift ops (GenericBitwiseOr, etc.) as
truncation points in the int32 truncation pass, not just Int32
variants.  Walk backward from these points to convert GenericAdd,
GenericSubtract, GenericMultiply, GenericIncrement, and
GenericDecrement to their wrapping Int32 counterparts when:
1. The node has exactly one non-transparent consumer (truncation chain)
2. All inputs are provably i32-valued (constants, Int32 ops, bitwise
   ops, Phi nodes in smi loops, or already-converted nodes)

This fixes the arithmetic_loop benchmark pattern:
  n = (n + i * 3 - 1) | 0
where the \| 0\ truncation makes all intermediate overflows
irrelevant.  Previously, the smi_guarded 32-bit ADD would deopt on
overflow (sum > 2^31), permanently blocking JIT after 10 retries.
Now the entire chain uses wrapping Int32 ops with no overflow check.

Also reverts the 64-bit smi_guarded fix (0bbe81fd) which caused a
325x regression in property_access/deep_object benchmarks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: rewrite SELECT * as FROM-first DuckDB syntax (#21)

Implements the FROM-first formatting rule.

## What

`SELECT * FROM t` → `FROM t` (and `WHERE`, `ORDER BY`, etc. are
preserved)

## How

- Added `prefer_from_first: bool = True` to `JarifyConfig`
- Overrides `select_sql` in `JarifyGenerator`: when the only expression
is `*` and there are no JOINs, strips the `SELECT` line
- JOINs excluded: sqlglot re-parses `FROM func() JOIN` by attaching the
join to the table node rather than the Select, causing an idempotency
shift — keeping `SELECT *` for joined queries avoids this

## Tests

- `tests/fixtures/select/from_first` — simple, WHERE, ORDER BY cases
- `TestFromFirst` — star converts, JOIN keeps SELECT, DISTINCT keeps
SELECT, explicit columns unaffected, opt-out via config

Resolves #20

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
CRITICAL FIX: Run ALL 3 TASKS in inference.py for validator verification

Issue: Validator needs to verify all 3 graders are working. Previous inference.py
only ran one task (TASK_NAME default), so validator couldn't see 3 graders being used.

Solution: Modified inference.py to:
- Loop through all 3 tasks: fix_logic_bug, fix_algorithm_bug, optimize_and_fix
- Run each task independently with full step loop
- Emit separate [START]/[STEP]/[END] blocks for each task
- Print [SUMMARY] showing all 3 tasks validated with scores

Result: Validator now sees:
- 3 [START] lines (one per task)
- Multiple [STEP] lines (one per step in each task)
- 3 [END] lines (one per task with scores)
- [SUMMARY] confirming all 3 graders executed successfully

This ensures validator can verify 'scores/reward in 0.0-1.0 range' for ALL 3 tasks.

fix: replace hardcoded reroute telemetry values in Athena pre-batched dispatch path (#292)

In the Athena pre-batched dispatch path, the specialist reroute telemetry
block was emitting three hardcoded signal values:
  - fillRatio: 1.0  (never reflects actual token fill)
  - laneScore: 0.5  (neutral default, ignores lane performance history)
  - adaptiveFillThreshold: 1.0  (ignores lane quality adjustment)

These values fed into reroute_history.jsonl and downstream optimizer
penalty feedback loops with no useful signal, polluting scoreboard data
and weakening the adaptive threshold cycle.

Fix: compute each value from existing in-scope signals:
  - fillRatio: actual summed estimatePlanTokens for the rerouted role
    group divided by getUsableModelContextTokens (mirrors buildTokenFirstBatches)
  - laneScore: getLaneScore(lanePerformanceFromCycle, lane)
  - adaptiveFillThreshold: computeAdaptiveSpecialistFillThreshold using
    lanePerformanceFromCycle and DEFAULT_SPECIALIST_FILL_THRESHOLD

Also restructures the per-plan loop into a per-role-group map so each
rerouted specialist role emits one telemetry record, matching the
convention established by buildTokenFirstBatches.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: two bugs in ART board selector

1. ReferenceError crash in adminHubLoadArtBoardsByProject: the variable
   'btn' was used before its var declaration (which was accidentally
   omitted in the previous edit). JavaScript does not hoist across function
   scopes so this threw ReferenceError on line 1, crashing the function
   before the API call fired. Typed project key never triggered board load.

2. Project key auto-fill used selectedOption.dataset.projectkey which can
   be unreliable for options built via innerHTML. Switched to the more
   explicit getAttribute('data-projectkey'). Also removed the 'only fill
   if empty' guard — when a board is selected from a name search the
   project key should always be written (the old guard prevented this if
   the user had typed anything in the field previously).

commit.message:
fix: ghost rendering at canvas edges for true torus look (closes #9.2)

Particles near a canvas edge were teleporting to the other side, breaking
the toroidal-space illusion. Fix: render ghost copies at the wrapped position
whenever a particle is within RENDER_RADIUS (14 px) of any edge.

Implementation:
- drawParticleAt(p, x, y): core rendering at arbitrary position
- drawParticle(p): builds xs/ys arrays, cross-product ensures all 4 edges
  and all 4 corners are handled correctly (up to 4 draws per particle)
- RENDER_RADIUS = 14 derived from outer glow diameter (28px / 2)

Bond lines across the boundary remain skipped; that is the next step.

  ERROR: hardcoded API keys, tokens, passwords, bot tokens,
